metadata.description: Spaced repetition flashcards for software engineering concepts

cards.back: List files and directories in current location
Change directory to specified path
Print working directory (show current location)
Create a new directory
Remove/delete a file (use -r for directories)
Copy file or directory
Move or rename file/directory
Display file contents
Send output of one command as input to another
Redirect output to a file (overwrites)
Append output to a file
Make a file executable
Initialize a new git repository
Clone a remote repository
Show working tree status (staged, modified, untracked)
Stage changes for commit
Commit staged changes with a message
Upload local commits to remote repository
Fetch and merge changes from remote
Create a new branch
Switch to a different branch
Merge another branch into current branch
Show commit history
Show changes between commits or working tree
Show all HEAD movements—your safety net for 'lost' commits
Reapply commits on top of another branch (linear history)
Temporarily save uncommitted changes
Apply a specific commit to current branch
Undo last commit, keep changes staged
Undo last commit, discard changes (dangerous!)
Create a new commit that undoes a previous commit
File specifying which files git should ignore
Application Programming Interface—how software components communicate
Representational State Transfer—common API architectural style
Request method for retrieving data
Request method for creating new resources
Request method for updating resources
Request method for removing resources
Success—request completed successfully
Bad Request—client error in request
Unauthorized—authentication required
Not Found—requested resource doesn't exist
Internal Server Error—something went wrong on server
JavaScript Object Notation—common data format for APIs

Activate virtual environment (Unix/Mac)
Install a package in current environment
List installed packages with versions
File listing project dependencies
Modern Python project configuration file
Install package in editable/development mode

Install the build tool for creating packages
Build a distributable package (wheel and sdist)
Run all tests in current directory
Run tests with verbose output
Run tests in specific file
Run tests matching name pattern
Decorator for reusable test setup functions
Basic assertion—fails if not equal
Context manager to test that an exception is raised
Run tests with coverage report
File for sharing fixtures across test files
Decorator to run test with multiple input sets
Auto-format Python code consistently
Lint Python code for errors and style issues
Format code with ruff (alternative to black)
Sort import statements consistently
Check type hints for errors
Tool to run checks automatically before commits
Don't Repeat Yourself—avoid code duplication
Keep It Simple, Stupid—prefer simple solutions
Surface indication of potential deeper problem in code
Cost of shortcuts taken now that must be paid later
Define a new class
Constructor method—initializes new instances
Reference to the current instance of the class
Call a method on an instance
Decorator to make a method act like an attribute
Method that doesn't need instance or class reference
Method that receives the class as first argument
Inheritance—Child inherits from Parent
Call a method from the parent class
Return string representation for print()
Return unambiguous string representation
'Has-a' relationship—object contains other objects
Proposal to merge changes, enables code review
Personal copy of someone else's repository
Track bugs, features, and tasks on GitHub
Process of examining code for quality and correctness
Looks Good To Me—approving a PR
Work-in-progress PR not ready for review
Combine all PR commits into one before merging
Rules requiring reviews/checks before merging to main
File defining who reviews changes to specific paths
Automated process defined in .github/workflows/*.yml
Set of steps that run on the same runner
Individual task within a job
Server that executes the workflow
Trigger workflow when code is pushed
Trigger workflow on PR events
Action to check out repository code
Action to set up Python environment
Execute a shell command in workflow
Make a job wait for another job to complete
Run job with multiple configurations (Python versions, OS)
String literal documenting a module/class/function
Triple-quoted string format for docstrings

cards.front: Type hint: def f(x: int) -> str:
cards.back: Annotate parameter and return types

Main documentation file for a repository
File documenting changes between versions
Guide for how to contribute to a project

Alternative to Sphinx—simpler, Markdown-based
